description: Tuesday morning and weГўв‚¬в„ўll be gone. So, instead of me not writing for another week, here I Гўв‚¬Е“yam,Гўв‚¬Вќ and hope you are the same. Oh yes, IГўв‚¬в„ўm on the front steps eating a choc. Hershey. Want some? DadГўв‚¬в„ўs in the country for services and MomГўв‚¬в„ўs in the house visiting with an old negro lady who had dinner with us. Her mother was a slave in the south who married a half negro & came north to farm over 50 yrs. ago. SheГўв‚¬в„ўs very negro-like, and lovely as can be. Bernetta is still in England and says she will probably be there for another month, which suits us ok. Say, guess who I saw on Friday? HeГўв‚¬в„ўs probably told you. Anyway I looked up with a plate of food for someone and there was Hans Froslee. You see, we were at a huge stock sale Гўв‚¬вЂњ Aberdeen Angus, pure bred Гўв‚¬вЂњ and the L. Aid was serving dinner so I helped. It was some sale, & I had a picnic watching it.

creator: Skillbred, Margaret
title: Page, ND 1b
description: Mr. Noble, the manager of the Hartley farms, sold 138 cows & 13 bulls & all averaged a little less that $800.00 a piece. The prize bull sold to a N. York man for $7500.00! I got a bang out of watching one white-haired old lady from Illinois Гўв‚¬вЂњ worth 14 million Гўв‚¬вЂњ sitting on the 1st bleacher bench chewing gum and bidding away like an old veteran, which she no doubt was. Mr. Froslee had a good time too, but he said he was broke so he couldnГўв‚¬в„ўt bid, but he sold some of his cattle to a Mont. man. HeГўв‚¬в„ўs awfully thin, isnГўв‚¬в„ўt he? His last sale must have been hard on him. Anyway, the sale was fun, & the L. Aid made $200.00 & Mr. Noble cleared about $98,000.00. Can you imagine N.D. having a place like that? IГўв‚¬в„ўm glad I donГўв‚¬в„ўt have to worry about what to do with $98,000 or 14 million. What a job! So far all IГўв‚¬в„ўve done is clean. IГўв‚¬в„ўm nearly through housecleaning the kitchen, back porch left. This place is much too big. But I should be glad, shouldnГўв‚¬в„ўt I, that it gives me something to do.

description: Oh, weГўв‚¬в„ўve mowed the lawn, which is terrifically tough, and pulled a few weeds. Dad has planted garden Гўв‚¬вЂњ potatoes & corn! Both are doing well, thank you. YouГўв‚¬в„ўve been doing more than clean, no doubt! Want some help? Dad loves to milk cows but I donГўв‚¬в„ўt know how the cows like it. He suggested that he stop off in Vining on his way home from St. Paul! DonГўв‚¬в„ўt get worried, I donГўв‚¬в„ўt think he will. And donГўв‚¬в„ўt worry, anyway. Both he and Mom are very nice about a lot of things. Blow, blow! I mean, I think you & they will get on all right. Has it rained? ItГўв‚¬в„ўs been warning and threatening all week but just a dreary drizzle. HereГўв‚¬в„ўs a good one, came over the radio: Marriage is like soup. When the spooning is over itГўв‚¬в„ўs cooled off. Hmm? Nope! Do please write and let me know how Minnesota gets along without a N.D. brat-like schoolmaГўв‚¬в„ўam. And tell me how your grandma is, and the rest of you. description: Page, N.D. June 24, 1945 somewhere around 4 oГўв‚¬в„ўclock Dear Sam, Another Sunday! And itГўв‚¬в„ўs lovely. What are you doing; sleeping, or drowsing with thoughts a-whirling, or gadding and gabbing. Me Гўв‚¬вЂњ IГўв‚¬в„ўm just sitting! For awhile I was on the lawn letting my legs get a bit of tan but theyГўв‚¬в„ўre red instead and it got too too warm so I moved into the porch. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs so quiet. description: 7 oГўв‚¬в„ўclock! I went to sleep like the rest of the household. Now I fell much more like writing. Dad came home just a bit ago. He has been to a Golden Wedding too, on the way home from Ortonville. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs still lovely outdoors and a wonderful evening for a lake ride. Boy! Wishful thinking, huh? The neighbors next door have been trying out the kidsГўв‚¬в„ў swing! The are both big people so I expected to hear a crack & a plop but none came. He farms with his son & he just said it was nice weather but even so the corn wonГўв‚¬в„ўt be anywhere knee high by the 4th. Is that supposed to be a standard Гўв‚¬вЂњ knee high by the 4th? Mr. Olson & I finished mowing & raking the lawn again last night. It was better this time, maybe because he sharpened the blades. He had a big blister on this thumb & I have 2, not on my thumbs but in the palms of my hands. ArenГўв‚¬в„ўt we tough! Oh me, In time a few callouses should develop & protect me Гўв‚¬вЂњ that is if IГўв‚¬в„ўm to keep up the yard which looks likely. There isnГўв‚¬в„ўt a thing to write about Page or its surroundings or its people so maybe IГўв‚¬в„ўd better bring this epistle to an end. I did write another page but it landed in the basket Гўв‚¬вЂњ I couldnГўв‚¬в„ўt make it out either. A storm was on the way and it looked like hail but now the sun is out again. IГўв‚¬в„ўm finding it awfully hard to write, so you arenГўв‚¬в„ўt the only one, if you do yet. I love you and thank God continually for what you are to me, and pray that I may be worthy of you when and if you want me. I pray too that He may so direct us that we will grow in Him each day and learn to do His will in everything. Now IГўв‚¬в„ўd better go and mail this before I read it though again & it goes into a basket or some such. IГўв‚¬в„ўve said what I truly wanted to say and found hard to say because IГўв‚¬в„ўm weak, I guess. Love, Marg

description: Vining, Minn. June 21, 1945 Dear Marg, I just finished washing my hair and shaving off 4 days beard. IГўв‚¬в„ўm going to see Skilbred tonight you know. Wish I were. Thanks much for your last letter, you donГўв‚¬в„ўt know how it makes me feel. Your praying that you would be worthy of me. After all who do you think I am. I pay to you know that through no fault of mine you should be sorry for tiring to me. YouГўв‚¬в„ўre [you her] and everything outside of God to me, and may He grant that our love should last forever. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs a beautiful night. The moon is hanging out over the lake. Would be perfect only if you were here. Things really are looking good now though even if the weather has been rather cool. Today was the first really warm day weГўв‚¬в„ўve had and the longest too of course. Makes the corn grow.

Well not much nore happens around here outside of milking cows, feeding pigs, cultivating corn and things like that there. So I better sign off now. Should tell you that the mailbox by the side of the road means much to me, these days. So you better keep Гўв‚¬Лњem coming. Sa for dwleve sa fra da. Love, Sam

title: Vining, MN 4a
description: Vining, Minn. June 26, 1945 Dear Marg, How are you? Fine I hope. Me, I was never much better. Still weigh 111 lbs. anyhow thatГўв‚¬в„ўs not so bad huh. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs thundering, lightening and raining all at once. Nice day. Oh well first rain for over a week so we shouldnГўв‚¬в„ўt bick[?]. Just cut the first hay today. So that will get nice and wet. Carl and I rented 50 acres of extra hay ground together. So we shouldnГўв‚¬в„ўt run out of work for a while at least. The corn just might be knee high by the 4th is the weather stays right. This knee high business is just and old rule if it isntГўв‚¬в„ў that far ahead by the 4th then itГўв‚¬в„ўs a poor chance it will be ripe corn unless we get a warm fall. Hi farmer. How you like all this farm stull. Better get used to it huh. IГўв‚¬в„ўm hoping you know.

title: Vining, MN 4b

But then we hope they did the right thing anyhow. The Rev. Carlson of B.L. is having services in the hall at Vining every eve. this week. Rude and I were in this eve. Rubye had L.D.R. out at BekkГўв‚¬в„ўs[?], So we took her back to Henning afterward. She hasnГўв‚¬в„ўt quit yet but she just might anytime I think. I hope anyhow. You said a while ago that you might go to Bible camp at Alex. You just wouldnГўв‚¬в„ўt come by at that time would you. Wish you could. IГўв‚¬в„ўd really like to see you thatГўв‚¬в„ўs for sure. Could be a few things IГўв‚¬в„ўl like to talk to you about you know. And then just seeing you again would help an awfull lot. Shorten the summer you know thatГўв‚¬в„ўs been long enough already.

description: Page, N.D. June 28, 1945 Dear Sam, Here it is, the last Thursday in June. CanГўв‚¬в„ўt believe it. And itГўв‚¬в„ўs cold. It rained this morning but turned out to be a warm afternoon but tonight is definitely cool. We had entertainment this afternoon Гўв‚¬вЂњ 2 little boys, one 10 & the other 7 yrs. old Гўв‚¬вЂњ stayed with us while their father went to Fargo. They came home from Bible School and stayed until about 9:30 tonite. They were nice but I think I played Hide and Seek too long & walked up town too much. IГўв‚¬в„ўm tired. And appetites! Oh my! They did away with 6 sandwiches apiece after school & a pile of cookies. They ran it off though.

description: We tried out the new stove on a choc. Cake & some cookies. These farmers supply us with gobs of cream. Now we have 4 qts. on hand. All sour. MomГўв‚¬в„ўs churning now. Nice farmers, what? Tomorrow we are invited to a coffee party in the county. 7 miles to drink coffee. Kinda dull but nice. Dad is stiff as a board tonight. He was in bed most of the day. His back is simply awful. Tomorrow heГўв‚¬в„ўs to go to Ladies Aid in the country & then go on to Bible Camp Гўв‚¬вЂњ 60 miles away. So heГўв‚¬в„ўs better perk up. He has too much driving to do for his age Гўв‚¬вЂњ and weight. This is the first day at home for him for over 2 wks. And sometimes I think I work!

creator: Halvorson, Selmer
title: Vining, MN 5a
description: Vining, Minn. July 2, 1945 Dear Marg, Just me again. Little behind time maybe, But here just the same. We were at church yesterday morning. Then company in the afternoon. And last night we took Chris. Along to church. We didnГўв‚¬в„ўt get back till 12 so I just went to bed without writing. Imagine Ruby really talked to Ken. about quiting So she will be home for a while at least. IГўв‚¬в„ўm going up to get her tomorrow night, luggage and all. She probably will take only a month or six weeks off. But thatГўв‚¬в„ўs going to help her a lot, us too I think. So now you had better decide to come us for a few days after Bible Camp or something. I will see to that you will make some sort of bus connections. Even if I have to take you to Fargo. The old dodge still limps and chugs a little. But he will still get us there, I hope.

title: Vining, MN 5b
description: How about that Buick, have you driven it yet. Or maybe your Dad wonГўв‚¬в„ўt take chances on it, hu. You really should learn though. Might come in handy. Of course I would still be glad to teach you, you know. Maybe the only thing I would know how to teach you. You shouldnГўв‚¬в„ўt work so hard. Get old quick that way. Me, I just monky around16-18 hrs. a day so that arenГўв‚¬в„ўt so bad for a little guy. We are going to that picnic the 4th. It will be down at GeorgeГўв‚¬в„ўs resort. You know that place? Hope we can have a softball game or something. Would give us some exercise. Might get stiff otherwise from laying around. I canГўв‚¬в„ўt write. So hope I will see you soon. Then maybe I could try do some talking or wonГўв‚¬в„ўt you let me. Night now. Love, Sam P.S. The day, well he would say Hello, if he knew enough. HeГўв‚¬в„ўs not so brilliant thatГўв‚¬в„ўs for sure.

title: Alexandria, MN 1a
description: Please: Excuse the pencil. Nothing else available. Mount Carmel Alexandria Tuesday Aft. Dear Sam, ItГўв‚¬в„ўs nearly time for supper but if I donГўв‚¬в„ўt start youГўв‚¬в„ўll never hear. We are really having a wonderful time and I do wish you could be here. Of course the nights are cold but the rain holds off so itГўв‚¬в„ўs nice. We havenГўв‚¬в„ўt tried the water yet. Last night we 3 slept together but Nora took the middle and baked all night & Rubye & I got cold so I moved out. Tonight we try it alone. The beds are good, the meals are good, the teachers are great, and everyone seems so nice. Could it be better? In the morning, classes begin at 9 and run till 12:15. In the afternoon you do as you please. Today we slept and went to a picnic at 4 oГўв‚¬в„ўclock that was meant to be a get-acquainted affair.

creator: Skillbred, Margaret
title: Alexandria, MN 1b
description: One hour in the mornings is on the study of Acts & the topic: Every Christian a Witness for Christ. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs a challenging topic and makes me feel how poorly IГўв‚¬в„ўve done. The 2nd hour is on Joseph and his life. This morning it was on his hardships & why he had to have them. During the 3rd hour missionaries take turns telling of their experiences. This morning a lady from China, who has adopted a little Chinese girl, spoke, and the little girl sang Jesus Loves Me, in Chinese. She has black curly hair & is as cute as a bugГўв‚¬в„ўs ear. We just got back from supper. Evening service is at 8:30 & lights are off at 11. So we have 2 hrs. of rest. Rest & more rest. CouldnГўв‚¬в„ўt you use some? Some day I hope you can. I think Rubye & Nora are writing too.

creator: Skillbred, Margaret
title: Alexandria, MN 1c
description: Did you stay on the road Sunday Eve.? Not that youГўв‚¬в„ўre a punk driver, but --. You know, someday IГўв‚¬в„ўll have to quit my giggling and shall we talk? Or donГўв‚¬в„ўt we need to? Of course IГўв‚¬в„ўm perfectly content to sit and listen to you, or just sit, as youГўв‚¬в„ўve noticed. I miss you, Sam and love you but I do so hope that both of us will put Christ first and grow in Him together. I know I do so many foolish things & say a lot more but over look them, will you, & IГўв‚¬в„ўll keep trying. We learned a chorus last evening at the service and it made me think of our sunsets, & wished that more people could sing with us. Want to hear it? He give me joy in place of sorrows He gives me love that casts out fear. He gives me sunshine in place of shadows And beauty for ashes here. We donГўв‚¬в„ўt know if we can stay for Sunday yet or not. They have a full camp for Sat. WeГўв‚¬в„ўll probably let you know when we decide to come. Thanks so very much for last weekend. GodГўв‚¬в„ўs peace till I see you Гўв‚¬вЂњ Yours, Marg

We eat and eat and IГўв‚¬в„ўm still hungry. This afternoon I had cookies & cake & 3 doughnuts & went to supper hungry. Must be the mountain air!

description: They have room so that we can stay until Sunday aft. Are you planning to come down for the day? Or donГўв‚¬в„ўt you know that Olga thought of a plan for doing that? description: Page, N.D. July 25, 1945 Dear Sam, Yes, IГўв‚¬в„ўm home again. And N.D. is just as hot as Minnesota. I came on the train yesterday morning and the folks were on hand to meet. They must have been lonesome, huh? Rev. Tang and I missed the bus from Fergus to Fargo by about 10 minutes. Everetta didnГўв‚¬в„ўt think they would pull out before we got in but they did, so Mr. Elliot took us to Detroit Lakes and we had 1Г‚Вѕ hr. wait there but got into Fargo 3 hrs. sooner than if weГўв‚¬в„ўd waited in Fergus. But was it hot! We had to stand part of the way from D.L. to Fargo so it was a relief to really get out into a cool depot. I called mother and got a very welcome reception and an apology for the hurried and sharp call of last Thursday. She was in a hurry; must have been. Anyway, all was well. Then I went to spend the night with a friend who has a basement apartment. Smart of me to pick a cool place, donГўв‚¬в„ўt you think? The train on Tues. morning was to leave at 7, but left at 8 so I didnГўв‚¬в„ўt have to hurry.

creator: Skillbred, Margaret
title: Page, ND 7b
description: We heard from Bernetta but she was still in France at that time, and expected to spend anywhere from 40-60 days on the water, so they must be going quite a way. Today we wash clothes. Yesterday we cleaned. Oh yes, I came home to find that we are moving again before Christmas! The parsonage is being remodeled finally so we move whenever it is ready. The crops are pretty fair, according to Pop. They begin harvesting next week. You should see the spuds & sweet corn in our back yard! They are really nice. The corn is nearly shoulder high. Must be the soil; canГўв‚¬в„ўt be the gardeners. Are you still stacking hay? Yes, I know, thereГўв‚¬в„ўs plenty of it. Have fun. Tell Carl hello, and Walt, if heГўв‚¬в„ўs still with you. Thanks so much, Sam, for the nice time. It was a wonderful time, so nice that I might come again next year! Now I gotta go scrub clothes. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs still mid-morning you see. Гўв‚¬ЛњBye for now. The 4th of Sept. will come sooner than I think, maybe. Love, Margaret

Say, the hail ruined whole sections around Buffalo last week. Most of Page county is all right. Even our corn is standing up again, and today Mom dug 2 small hills of potatoes and found 16 pretty good sized spuds. Dad was right proud.
